chunk_summary: Prevention counselling and national supplementation schedules focus on age-appropriate feeding, micronutrient supplementation, and illness prevention to address feeding difficulties and faltering growth in children.
learning_objectives: Understand age-appropriate IYCF practices, including exclusive breastfeeding and nutrient-dense complementary feeding.
Learn national supplementation schedules for anemia control, deworming, and Vitamin A prophylaxis.
Recognize the importance of feeding during and after illness to prevent sustained faltering growth.
key_facts: Exclusive breastfeeding is recommended for the first 6 months, followed by nutrient-dense complementary feeding.
Biweekly IFA supplementation is scheduled for children aged 6–59 months under anemia control programs.
Albendazole dosing for deworming: 200 mg for ages 1–2 years, 400 mg for ages 2–19 years.
Vitamin A prophylaxis includes periodic dosing in late infancy and six-monthly dosing through early childhood.
WHO guidelines emphasize dietary diversity, energy density, and responsive feeding practices.
reference_quote: The 2023 WHO complementary feeding guideline emphasizes dietary diversity and energy density alongside responsive feeding.
